skills: rule/condition matching (sign states a rule; only one named person satisfies it, others have irrelevant attributes)
seasonal/hemisphere inference from an activity clue (beach volleyball in England -> season)
functional exclamation in an emergency context (drowning, distinguishing similar-sounding phrases)
holiday-card interpretation (generic vs specific holiday wording)
object-clue inference (cutlery implies a specific food ordered)
universal-quantifier table matching ("All students..." requires every row/column checked)
superlative bar-chart/graph reading (highest/lowest across named categories)
chart construction from an arithmetic statement ("two more than last year")
multi-turn map direction reading (turn + distance + relative position)
reading comprehension - diary/personal recount passage (family size counting, date arithmetic, main idea, specific detail, inference)
picture-category identification (winter sport popular in western countries)
punctuation correctness (selecting the correct one this time, not the incorrect one)
dialogue completion across many everyday settings (20 items, items 21-40)
grammar: past simple vs future mismatch detection
modal verbs and conditionals (if you like it, I can lend it to you)
question_types: chart selection from an arithmetic statement
diary reading comprehension with 5 linked questions
short 2-line dialogue completion across many settings (20 items)
reading_patterns: first-person pandemic-era diary (distance learning from home): family members, subjects learned, chores, pet -> questions requiring precise counting of named people (excluding pets), date arithmetic from a stated interval ("in two days"), main idea, specific detail, and favourite-activity identification
dialogue_patterns: reason-giving exchange matched to a why-question
general-knowledge trivia embedded in dialogue (days in a week)
conditional offer exchange (if you like it, I can lend it to you)
unit-appropriate answer matching (speed in km/h, not kg or baht)
polite-attention-getting phrase for asking a stranger (excuse me) vs wrong-register alternatives
offering-to-help exchange from a picture context
distractor_patterns: condition-matching distractor: named person has an irrelevant attribute not related to the stated rule
comparison-direction / quantity swap in charts (2019 vs 2020 bars swapped)
tense/time-adverb mismatch distractor (wrong tense for the stated time marker)
wrong unit of measurement for the question asked (kg instead of km/h)
sequence-order violation distractor (steps given in wrong order)
literal homophone-like confusion for young learners (Help me! vs Help you!)
answer_logic: condition-matching items require checking a stated rule against multiple candidates, where distractors have plausible but irrelevant attributes
chart/graph items require reading two or more data points and applying the correct comparative/superlative direction
diary items require arithmetic on a stated date interval and precise counting of named entities, excluding non-human referents (pets)
tense items require matching the verb tense to an explicit time adverb in the same sentence
notes: Exam sat 13 March 2564 (BE+1 sitting-date convention). 40 items; items 1-20 standalone/reading, items 21-40 dialogue completion. First strongly pandemic-themed year (distance learning, hygiene) and first appearance of PM2.5/environmental topic and unit-of-measurement distractors.
